messages.content:
Project 2: Implement a gesture recognition algorithm for controlling a vacuum robot. The list of gestures are given below. Use the MATLAB GUI as the interface for interacting with the robot (drawing gestures). You need to record at least 10 samples per class to train the Rubine Classifier. Submit your training update GUI. Note: Don't forget to include the classification weights as a mat file. ( I got a minimum of 10 samples per each listed gestures in images directory).                     
B : Charge Battery
☐: Area Selection
C: Clean mop
O: Pause Operation
S: Start Operation
Sawtooth icon: Vacuum the surface
Glow plug indicator  : mop the surface                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
Project 3: In this, you will use the dataset Face.mat to classify
emotions, age, and gender of the subjects. This dataset includes 12 images of 6 different subjects. For
each subjects we have 2 samples of 6 different emotions. Use one of each samples in your training
dataset and the other one in your testing dataset. This way you would have 36 images in the training
dataset and 36 images in the testing dataset.
Goal: To classify the gender (2 classes: M, F), emotions (6 classes: angry, disgust, neutral, happy, sad,
surprised) and age (3 classes: Young, Mid age, Old). Using a linear classifier. You should label your
training and testing data for each of the classification problem separately.
Classifier: Use the linear classifier developed in Project 2. (You don’t need the Rubine Features)
Features: Projection of the image on each of the eigenfaces. For this purpose, you need to calculate
PCA of your training data. Your kth feature will be the projection (dot product) of the image on the
kth eigen vector (PCA direction) also known as eigenface.
Feature Selection: You will extract 36 Features as there are 36 images (and eigen vectors) in your
training data. Use the “sequentialfs” command in MATLAB to select the top 6 features using the
sequential forward search algorithm. For classification results, plot each of the testing image and list the predicted age, emotion and gender.
this purpose, plot each of the testing image and list the predicted age, emotion and gender.

For Project 2, A gesture recognition algorithm for controlling a vacuum robot:

1. Create a MATLAB GUI for drawing gestures and then train a Rubine Classifier using at least 10 samples per each gesture class. You can refer to this tutorial for implementing the Rubine Classifier: http://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/7648-dynamic-time-warping-dtw. Save the classification weights as a mat file.

2. Meanwhile, create labels for each gesture class (charge battery, area selection, clean mop, pause operation, start operation, vacuum surface, and mop surface).

For Project 3, emotion, age, and gender classification using PCA:

1. Divide the dataset into training and testing: 36 images in the training dataset and 36 images in the testing dataset.

2. Compute the PCA of the training data using the MATLAB function "eig". This will give the Eigenfaces (eigenvectors) and their corresponding eigenvalues.

3. Project each image on the Eigenfaces to obtain the projected feature vector. This can be done by taking the dot product of the image and each Eigenface.

4. Use the "sequentialfs" command in MATLAB to select the top 6 features using the sequential forward search algorithm.

5. Train a linear classifier (such as an SVM or logistic regression) using the selected features and the corresponding labels for age, emotion, and gender.

6. Test the classifier on the testing dataset, and for each testing image, display the predicted age, emotion, and gender.

Here's a rough code outline for Project 3:

```matlab
% Load the dataset
load('Face.mat');

% Divide the dataset into training and testing
[trainData, testData, trainLabels, testLabels] = ... % your function to split the data

% Calculate PCA on the training dataset
[EigVec, EigVal] = eig(cov(trainData));

% Project the images onto the Eigenfaces
projTrainData = trainData * EigVec;
projTestData = testData * EigVec;

% Select top 6 features using 'sequentialfs'
selection = sequentialfs(@yourClassifierFitFunc, projTrainData, trainLabels);

% Train the linear classifier using the selected features
% Your classifier can be logistic regression, SVM, etc.
classifier = trainClassifier(projTrainData(:, selection), trainLabels);

% Test the classifier on the testing data
predictions = testClassifier(classifier, projTestData(:, selection));

% Plot each testing image and list the predicted age, emotion, and gender
for i = 1:length(predictions)
    displayImage(testData(i, :));
    title(['Age: ', predictions(i).age, ', Emotion: ', predictions(i).emotion, ', Gender: ', predictions(i).gender]);
end
```

The above code is just a reference for you to understand the steps involved in the process. You need to replace the placeholders and implement the necessary functions for data splitting, classifier training, and testing. I hope this helps you with both projects! Good luck reaching your goals!

Can you implement the matlab code for the following : A gesture recognition algorithm for controlling a vacuum robot:

1. Create a MATLAB GUI for drawing gestures and creating labels for each gesture class (charge battery, area selection, clean mop, pause operation, start operation, vacuum surface, and mop surface).
2. Then Implement the Rubine Classifier and train it using at least 10 samples per each gesture class. Save the classification weights as a mat file.

As far as I can check , You've changed a single thing in the code I've given expect new button creation code. So, I want you to modify the code in such a way that the intial draw area I see should be fixed. The intial draw area is enough for the gestures I'm asked to draw. So, rewrite the code to make the draw screen fixed and also correct the scrolling motion. I can see some uncessary lines been drawn even for a small tap.

Sorry I forgot to mention the variable names of the dataset 'Face.mat'.>> whos('-file', 'Face.mat')
  Name           Size               Bytes  Class     Attributes

  II        100820x72            58072320  double              
  m              1x72                 576  double              . Based on this can you modify the code.
